quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Critical point"?

options.text: Average value has the same units as f(x); the (1/(b-a)) factor divides out the length units
Vertical: where denominator = 0 (numerator ≠ 0); horizontal: limits at ±∞; oblique: when degree(num) = degree(den) + 1
When f''(c) = 0, the critical point could be a max, min, or inflection point (e.g., f(x) = x⁴ has a min at x = 0 but f''(0) = 0)
An interior point c where f'(c) = 0 (horizontal tangent) or f'(c) does not exist (corner, cusp, or vertical tangent)

quiz_questions.explanation: "Critical point" is defined as: An interior point c where f'(c) = 0 (horizontal tangent) or f'(c) does not exist (corner, cusp, or vertical tangent). The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Absolute (global) extrema"?

options.text: The largest and smallest values of f on the entire domain; on [a,b], compare f at critical points and endpoints
If f'(x) = 0 for all x in (a,b), then f is constant on [a,b]; proven using MVT
If f'(c) = 0 and f''(c) > 0: local minimum (concave up); f''(c) < 0: local maximum (concave down)
The + C in every indefinite integral accounts for the fact that F(x) + C has the same derivative for any constant C

quiz_questions.explanation: "Absolute (global) extrema" is defined as: The largest and smallest values of f on the entire domain; on [a,b], compare f at critical points and endpoints. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Local (relative) extrema"?

options.text: P(t) = K/(1 + Ae^(-rt)) where A = (K-P₀)/P₀; produces an S-shaped (sigmoidal) curve
Uses the midpoint of each subinterval: M_n = Σf((xᵢ + xᵢ₊₁)/2)Δx; generally more accurate than left/right
f(c) is a local max if f(c) ≥ f(x) for all x near c; local min if f(c) ≤ f(x) for all x near c
When f''(c) = 0, the critical point could be a max, min, or inflection point (e.g., f(x) = x⁴ has a min at x = 0 but f''(0) = 0)

quiz_questions.explanation: "Local (relative) extrema" is defined as: f(c) is a local max if f(c) ≥ f(x) for all x near c; local min if f(c) ≤ f(x) for all x near c. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Closed interval method"?

options.text: First derivative test: always works but requires sign analysis on an interval; second derivative test: simpler but can be inconclusive
Motion along a straight line (one dimension); described completely by position as a function of time s(t)
Find dy/dt given dx/dt (or vice versa) by differentiating an equation relating x and y with respect to time t
To find absolute extrema on [a,b]: 1) find critical points, 2) evaluate f at critical points and endpoints, 3) compare values

quiz_questions.explanation: "Closed interval method" is defined as: To find absolute extrema on [a,b]: 1) find critical points, 2) evaluate f at critical points and endpoints, 3) compare values. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: In the context of Extreme Values and Critical Points, which best defines "Extreme Value Theorem"?

options.text: First derivative test: always works but requires sign analysis on an interval; second derivative test: simpler but can be inconclusive
A continuous function on a closed interval [a,b] must attain both an absolute maximum and an absolute minimum
Find dy/dt given dx/dt (or vice versa) by differentiating an equation relating x and y with respect to time t
∫csc²x dx = -cot x + C; ∫sec x tan x dx = sec x + C; ∫csc x cot x dx = -csc x + C

quiz_questions.explanation: In Extreme Values and Critical Points, "Extreme Value Theorem" refers to: A continuous function on a closed interval [a,b] must attain both an absolute maximum and an absolute minimum.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "An interior point c where f'(c) = 0 (horizontal tangent) or f'(c) does not exist (corner, cusp, or vertical tangent)"?

options.text: Verifying a solution
Tangent lines to implicit curves
Critical point
Lorenz curve

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Critical point".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "The largest and smallest values of f on the entire domain; on [a,b], compare f at critical points and endpoints"?

options.text: Combined technique
d/dx[cos x] = -sin x
Displacement vs. distance
Absolute (global) extrema

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Absolute (global) extrema".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "f(c) is a local max if f(c) ≥ f(x) for all x near c; local min if f(c) ≤ f(x) for all x near c"?

options.text: Removable discontinuity
Second derivative
Local (relative) extrema
Verifying a solution

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Local (relative) extrema".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "To find absolute extrema on [a,b]: 1) find critical points, 2) evaluate f at critical points and endpoints, 3) compare values"?

options.text: Infinite limit
Closed interval method
Table-based accumulation
Implicit differentiation with time

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Closed interval method".

quiz_questions.question_text: In Extreme Values and Critical Points, which term matches: "A continuous function on a closed interval [a,b] must attain both an absolute maximum and an absolute minimum"?

options.text: Implicit differentiation with time
Extreme Value Theorem
Related rates method
Variable exponent functions

quiz_questions.explanation: In Extreme Values and Critical Points, this refers to "Extreme Value Theorem".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about Extreme Values and Critical Points is accurate?

options.text: Implicit Differentiation finds dy/dx when y is not explicitly solved as a function of x.
A Riemann sum divides [a,b] into n subintervals and approximates the area using rectangles: Σᵢf(xᵢ*)Δx.
The inflection point occurs at P = K/2, where the population is growing fastest.
Extreme Values and Critical Points identifies where functions achieve their highest and lowest values.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Extreme Values and Critical Points as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which statement correctly describes an aspect of Extreme Values and Critical Points?

options.text: Success requires clear justification of setup, correct antiderivatives, appropriate use of technology, and precise interpretation of results in the given context.
As n→∞, Riemann sums converge to the definite integral: ∫ₐᵇf(x)dx = lim(n→∞) Σf(xᵢ*)Δx.
The indefinite integral ∫f(x)dx = F(x) + C, where C is the constant of integration (since derivatives of constants are zero).
The Extreme Value Theorem guarantees that a continuous function on [a,b] has both an absolute maximum and an absolute minimum.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Extreme Values and Critical Points as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which is true specifically about Extreme Values and Critical Points?

options.text: Implicit Differentiation finds dy/dx when y is not explicitly solved as a function of x.
Critical points (where f'(x) = 0 or f'(x) is undefined) are candidates for local extrema.
Solution curves follow the direction of these segments.
It is the theoretical foundation for many calculus results.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Extreme Values and Critical Points as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best explains a key idea of Extreme Values and Critical Points?

options.text: The inflection point occurs at P = K/2, where the population is growing fastest.
Derivatives represent rates of change in any context: population growth rates, velocity, temperature change rates, marginal economic quantities, and reaction rates.
If f'(x) > 0 on an interval, then f is increasing on that interval; if f'(x) < 0, f is decreasing.
To find absolute extrema on [a,b]: evaluate f at all critical points in (a,b) and at both endpoints; the largest value is the absolute max, the smallest is the absolute min.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Extreme Values and Critical Points as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which accurately reflects the content on Extreme Values and Critical Points?

options.text: Extreme Values and Critical Points identifies where functions achieve their highest and lowest values.
The power rule: d/dx[xⁿ] = nxⁿ⁻¹ for any real n.
These are important for integration and solving applied problems.
It is the theoretical foundation for many calculus results.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Extreme Values and Critical Points as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following is a real-world application of Extreme Values and Critical Points?

options.text: Hydrology: ∫(inflow rate - outflow rate)dt gives the net change in reservoir water level over a drought period
Error propagation bounds: if a measuring instrument's response function has bounded derivative, MVT limits the maximum output error from a given input error
Temperature modeling: since temperature changes continuously, the IVT guarantees that between any two measured temperatures, every intermediate value was achieved
Mountain hiking: the highest point on a trail (absolute max) and the lowest point (absolute min) are found by checking peaks, valleys, and the trail endpoints

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Extreme Values and Critical Points.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which example demonstrates the practical relevance of Extreme Values and Critical Points?

options.text: Hydrology: ∫(inflow rate - outflow rate)dt gives the net change in reservoir water level over a drought period
Manufacturing optimization: finding the production level that yields the absolute maximum profit on a feasible interval [0, capacity]
Error propagation bounds: if a measuring instrument's response function has bounded derivative, MVT limits the maximum output error from a given input error
Rocket propulsion: as fuel is burned, the rocket becomes lighter, changing the force equation—integration handles the continuously varying mass

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Extreme Values and Critical Points.

quiz_questions.question_text: How is Extreme Values and Critical Points applied in real-world contexts?

options.text: Radioactive decay: dN/dt = -λN separates to dN/N = -λdt, giving N = N₀e^(-λt)—the exponential decay model
Structural engineering: identifying the point of maximum stress on a beam (critical point of the stress function) to prevent failure
AP Calculus free-response questions consistently test the ability to set up integrals from verbal descriptions and geometric reasoning
Radiocarbon dating: ¹⁴C decays with t₁/₂ ≈ 5,730 years; measuring remaining ¹⁴C in organic material determines its age up to ~50,000 years

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Extreme Values and Critical Points.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which scenario illustrates the principles of Extreme Values and Critical Points?

options.text: Oil spill spreading: a circular oil slick expanding on water has dA/dt = 2πr·dr/dt, used to estimate cleanup time and containment needs
Velocity as the derivative of position: v(t) = s'(t) tells you the instantaneous speed and direction of a moving object at any moment
Moore's Law: the roughly exponential growth of transistor density in microchips (doubling every ~2 years) eventually creating unforeseen limits in physics
Mountain hiking: the highest point on a trail (absolute max) and the lowest point (absolute min) are found by checking peaks, valleys, and the trail endpoints

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Extreme Values and Critical Points.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best shows Extreme Values and Critical Points in practice?

options.text: Error analysis in numerical methods: understanding limits helps predict how rounding errors accumulate as step sizes approach zero in computer calculations
A ball thrown upward returns to its starting height: Rolle's Theorem guarantees it had zero velocity (was momentarily at rest) at some point during its flight
Manufacturing optimization: finding the production level that yields the absolute maximum profit on a feasible interval [0, capacity]
AP Calculus free-response questions consistently test the ability to set up integrals from verbal descriptions and geometric reasoning

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Extreme Values and Critical Points.
